name: Salted Herbs
recipeInstructions:
These seasonings seem to be added to many traditional French-Canadian recipes. "Herbs preserved with vegetables and salt make a lively seasoning for soups, particularly pea soup; sauces, stews, and omelettes." A commercial brand, Les Herbes Salees du bas du fleuve, is marketed by J.Y. Roy of St. Flavie, Quebec. This recipe comes from the Metis district.
1. In a large bowl, combine herbs and vegetables.
2. Layer 1 inch of herb mixture in the bottom of a crock or glass bowl and sprinkle with some salt.
3. Repeat layers until all of the herb mixture and salt is used.
4. Cover and refrigerate for 2 weeks.
5. Drain off accumulated liquid and pack herb mixture into sterilized jars.
6. Refrigerate until ready to use. Makes about 5 to 6 cups.
